perturbation.original_text: 6.11 Audit. AbbVie shall, shall cause its Affiliates to, and shall use commercially reasonable efforts to cause its Sublicensees to, keep complete and accurate books and records pertaining to Net Sales of Licensed Products, in sufficient detail to calculate all amounts payable hereunder. At the request of Harpoon, AbbVie shall permit an independent public accounting firm of nationally recognized standing designated by Harpoon and reasonably acceptable to AbbVie, [***], to audit the books and records maintained pursuant to this Section 6.11 to ensure the accuracy of all reports and payments made hereunder, including any permitted deductions from Net Sales pursuant to Section 1.108. Such examinations may not (a) be conducted for any [***] [***] (b) be conducted more than once in any [***] period or (c) be [***] (unless a previous audit revealed an underpayment with respect to such [***]). The accounting firm shall disclose to Harpoon only whether the reports are correct or not, and the specific details concerning any discrepancies. No other information shall be shared. Except as provided below, the cost of this audit shall be borne by Harpoon, unless the audit reveals a variance [***] from the reported amounts or [***], in which case AbbVie shall bear the cost of the audit.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.11 Audit. AbbVie shall keep books and records pertaining to Net Sales of Licensed Products. At the request of Harpoon, AbbVie may permit an audit of the books and records maintained pursuant to this Section 6.11 to ensure the accuracy of all reports and payments made hereunder. Such examinations may not be conducted more than once in any [***] period. The accounting firm shall disclose to Harpoon only whether the reports are correct or not, and the specific details concerning any discrepancies. No other information shall be shared. The cost of this audit shall be borne by Harpoon.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the requirements for AbbVie to (1) cause its Affiliates and use efforts to cause its Sublicensees to keep complete books, (2) the standard that books should be kept in 'sufficient detail to calculate all amounts payable hereunder,' (3) the requirement to permit an audit. The law requires companies to maintain accurate books and records and to allow audits. This change significantly weakens Harpoon's ability to verify sales and royalty payments.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Keeping and furnishing information; rules and regulations
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts 15 U.S.C. § 78m, which mandates accurate record-keeping and reporting requirements for publicly traded companies. By weakening the audit clause, it reduces Harpoon's ability to ensure AbbVie maintains and reports accurate sales data, potentially impacting the accuracy of financial reporting related to this agreement.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Periodical and other reports | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Periodical and other reports U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78 l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security— (1) such information and documents (and such copies thereof) as the Commission shall require to keep reasonably current the information and documents required to be included in or filed with an application or registration statement filed pursuant to section 78l of this title , except that the Commission may not require the filing of any material contract wholly executed before July 1, 1962 . (2) such annual reports (and such copies thereof), certified if required by the rules and regulations of the Commission by independent public accountants, and such quarterly reports (and such copies thereof), as the Commission may prescribe. Every issuer of a security registered on a national securities exchange shall also file a duplicate original of such information, documents, and reports with the exchange . In any registration statement, periodic report, or other reports to be filed with the Commission , an emerging growth company need not present selected financial data in accordance with section 229.301 of title 17, Code of Federal Regulations, for any period prior to the earliest audited period presented in connection with its first registration statement that became effective under this chapter or the Securities Act of 1933 [ 15 U.S.C. 77a et seq.] and, with respect to any such statement or reports, an emerging growth company may not be required to
perturbation.justification: The modified contract omits essential requirements related to record-keeping and audit rights, creating a legal loophole. The original text requires AbbVie to 'cause its Affiliates to, and shall use commercially reasonable efforts to cause its Sublicensees to, keep complete and accurate books and records,' whereas the changed text only states AbbVie shall keep books. Additionally, the original text stipulates that 'AbbVie shall permit an independent public accounting firm...to audit the books and records,' whereas the changed text states AbbVie 'may permit an audit'. This weakening of the audit clause contradicts the intent of 15 U.S.C. § 78m, as outlined in the Law Explanation, which mandates accurate record-keeping and reporting requirements, because it reduces Harpoon's ability to ensure AbbVie maintains and reports accurate sales data, potentially impacting the accuracy of financial reporting. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states that 'Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78 l of this title shall file with the Commission...such information and documents...as the Commission shall require to keep reasonably current the information and documents'. Removing the requirement for AbbVie to permit an audit reduces Harpoon's ability to ensure the accuracy of AbbVie's filings.

perturbation.original_text: 5.8 Exclusivity with Respect to the Territory. 5.8.1 Harpoon shall not, and shall cause its Affiliates not to (a) directly or indirectly, develop, commercialize or otherwise exploit any Competing Product in any country or other jurisdiction in the Territory, or (b) license, authorize, appoint, or otherwise enable any Third Party to directly or indirectly, develop, commercialize or otherwise exploit any Competing Product in any country or other jurisdiction in the Territory, except, in each case ((a) and (b)), as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.8 Exclusivity with Respect to the Territory. 5.8.1 Harpoon shall not develop, commercialize or otherwise exploit any Competing Product in any country or other jurisdiction in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the requirement that Harpoon cause its Affiliates to not develop, commercialize, or exploit any competing products, and the requirement that Harpoon not 'license, authorize, appoint, or otherwise enable any Third Party' to do so. This significantly weakens the exclusivity granted to AbbVie, opening the door for Harpoon to contract with a third party to create a competing product. In effect, this makes the exclusivity provision very weak and creates risk for AbbVie.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 1 - Trusts, etc., in restraint of trade illegal; penalty
perturbation.law_explanation: By significantly weakening the exclusivity clause, this modification introduces potential antitrust concerns under 15 U.S.C. § 1. While the original clause aimed to prevent Harpoon from enabling competition, the modified version might be seen as an unreasonable restraint of trade if it allows Harpoon to circumvent exclusivity through affiliates or third-party arrangements, undermining the spirit of the agreement's intended market exclusivity for AbbVie.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 1 - Trusts, etc., in restraint of trade illegal; penalty | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 1 - Trusts, etc., in restraint of trade illegal; penalty U.S. Code Notes prev | next Every contract, combination in the form of trust or otherwise, or conspiracy, in restraint of trade or commerce among the several States, or with foreign nations, is declared to be illegal. Every person who shall make any contract or engage in any combination or conspiracy hereby declared to be illegal shall be deemed guilty of a felony, and, on conviction thereof, shall be punished by fine not exceeding $100,000,000 if a corporation, or, if any other person, $1,000,000, or by imprisonment not exceeding 10 years, or by both said punishments, in the discretion of the court. (July 2, 1890, ch. 647, § 1, 26 Stat. 209 ; Aug. 17, 1937, ch. 690, title VIII, 50 Stat. 693 ; July 7, 1955, ch. 281, 69 Stat. 282 ; Pub. L. 93–528, § 3 , Dec. 21, 1974 , 88 Stat. 1708 ; Pub. L. 94–145, § 2 , Dec. 12, 1975 , 89 Stat. 801 ; Pub. L. 101–588, § 4(a) , Nov. 16, 1990 , 104 Stat. 2880 ; Pub. L. 108–237, title II, § 215(a) , June 22, 2004 , 118 Stat. 668 .) Editorial Notes Amendments 2004— Pub. L. 108–237 substituted “$100,000,000” for “$10,000,000”, “$1,000,000” for “$350,000”, and “10” for “three”. 1990— Pub. L. 101–588 substituted “$10,000,000” for “one million dollars” and “$350,000” for “one hundred thousand dollars”. 1975— Pub. L. 94–145 struck out from first sentence two provisos granting anti-trust exemption to State fair trade laws. 1974— Pub. L. 93–528 substituted “a felony, and, on conviction thereof, shall be punished by fine not exceeding one million dollars if a corporation, or, if any other person, one hundred thousand dollars, or by imprisonment not exceeding three years” for “a misdemeanor, and on conviction thereof, shall be punished by fine not exceeding f
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes key provisions that prevent Harpoon from circumventing the exclusivity agreement through affiliates or third parties. This omission contradicts the spirit of 15 U.S.C. § 1, which prohibits contracts that restrain trade. The original clause ensured comprehensive exclusivity for AbbVie, but the changed text allows Harpoon to potentially enable competition, undermining the intended market exclusivity. As Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2 both state: 'Every contract, combination in the form of trust or otherwise, or conspiracy, in restraint of trade or commerce among the several States, or with foreign nations, is declared to be illegal.' By removing the limitations on Harpoon's affiliates and third-party dealings, the modified contract potentially enables such illegal restraint of trade.
